data.title: Germany – Saxovent into Milvio RTB solar
data.description:
By Cristiana Sandeva

Berlin-based renewables developer Saxovent Renewables is buying an 81.8MW ready-to-build solar project in Mecklenburg-West Pomerania from Hamburg-based Milvio Energy. Both developers are family-owned businesses by local founders. The project's revenues are partially backed by a government subsidy covering 20MW. Milvio Energy received legal support by RST Rechtsanwalte while Capcora was financial adviser.

data.title: Diesel margins slip $2 week on week; Jan spot activity underway
data.description:
Asia's diesel markets softened in most parts of this week, with timespreads and cash premiums both declining from last week amid expectations of easing supply tightness regionally in the next few weeks.

Refiner sales activity took centre stage for the week, with one major exporter selling off some January cargoes at slight premiums linked to free on board (FOB) Singapore quotes.

More refiner sales are expected in the coming days, as the usual suspects start offering their regular requirements.

Traders were also awaiting news on 2026 export quotas from China, with some quota swaps for the year-end said to be ongoing this week among the major refiners.

East-west spreads recovered to wider discounts of around $45 per ton, tracking firmness in ICE gasoil futures in the afternoon.

Refining margins (GO10SGCKMc1) continued to hold their ground at $20.8-20.9 a barrel, similar to the last two trading sessions, though values were $2 lower from last Friday's closing.

Declining further and hovering at two-month lows, the 10ppm sulphur gasoil cash differentials (GO10-SIN-DIF) closed the trading session at $1.08 a barrel.

Meanwhile, kerosene demand in the northeast Asia region is expected to firm up further given the colder weather, according to several trade sources, though shaky arbitrage spreads with the west could limit any price firmness.

Regrade (JETREG10SGMc1) continued fluctuating at premiums of 30-40 cents per barrel.

SINGAPORE CASH DEALS

- No deal for gasoil or jet fuel

INVENTORIES

- Gasoline stocks independently held in the Amsterdam-Rotterdam-Antwerp (ARA) refining and storage hub climbed by 5% on the week, data from Dutch consultancy Insights Global showed on Thursday.

NEWS

- Saudi Arabia has cut the January Arab Light crude oil official selling price to Asia to $0.60 a barrel above the Oman/Dubai average, its lowest level in five years, according to a pricing document reviewed by Reuters, amid growing signs of surplus in the oil market.

- State refiners Indian Oil Corp and Bharat Petroleum Corp have placed January orders for the loading of Russian oil from non-sanctioned suppliers due to widening discounts, trade sources with knowledge of the matter said.

- China's exports likely returned to growth in November after an unexpected dip in October, as manufacturers rushed to move inventory to take advantage of a fresh tariff truce with the U.S.

data.title: S&P/ASX 200 Benchmark Index Rises 0.24% This Week to 8634.60 — Data Talk
data.description:
The S&P/ASX 200 Benchmark Index is up 20.53 points or 0.24% this week to 8634.60

  • Up for two consecutive weeks
  • Up 218.10 points or 2.59% over the last two weeks
  • Largest two-week point and percentage gain since the week ending Aug. 15, 2025
  • Today it is up 16.16 points or 0.19%
  • Up for four consecutive trading days
  • Up 69.40 points or 0.81% over the last four trading days
  • Largest four-day point and percentage gain since Friday, Nov. 28, 2025
  • Longest winning streak since Thursday, Nov. 27, 2025, when the market rose for four straight trading days
  • Up eight of the past 10 trading days
  • Off 5.06% from its record close of 9094.70 hit Tuesday, Oct. 21, 2025
  • Highest closing value since Monday, Nov. 17, 2025
  • Off 5.06% from its 52-week high of 9094.71 hit Tuesday, Oct. 21, 2025
  • Up 17.59% from its 52-week low of 7343.25 hit Monday, April 7, 2025
  • Rose 2.54% from 52 weeks ago
  • Off 5.06% from its 2025 closing high of 9094.71 hit Tuesday, Oct. 21, 2025
  • Up 17.59% from its 2025 closing low of 7343.25 hit Monday, April 7, 2025
  • Year-to-date it is up 475.46 points or 5.83%

Source: Dow Jones Market Data, FactSet

data.title: Gold rises on weaker dollar as investors brace for US data
data.description:
Key points:
  • Silver headed for weekly gain
  • US dollar near five-week low
  • PCE inflation data for September due later today

By Pablo Sinha

Gold gained on Friday, supported by a softer dollar and growing bets on a U.S. interest rate cut, with markets focused on upcoming U.S. inflation data ahead of the Federal Reserve’s policy meeting next week.

Spot gold rose 0.4% to $4,225.11 per ounce, as of 1017 GMT, but was on track for a 0.1% weekly decline.

U.S. gold futures for February delivery edged 0.3% higher to $4,255.90 per ounce.

The dollar was languishing not far from a five-week low against major peers, making gold more affordable for holders of other currencies.

"Gold remains somewhat buoyed by market bets that the Fed will cut rates next week and a weaker dollar," said Lukman Otunuga, senior research analyst at FXTM.

Data on Wednesday showed that private payrolls fell by 32,000 in November, the sharpest drop in more than 2-1/2 years, while new U.S. unemployment benefit applications dropped to the lowest level in more than three years, weekly data showed on Thursday.

A majority of more than 100 economists polled by Reuters forecast the Fed will reduce its key interest rate by 25 basis points at its December 9-10 meeting.

Lower interest rates tend to favour non-yielding assets such as gold.

Investors are eyeing the delayed September PCE inflation data due later today, which will mark the final data release before next week’s FOMC meeting.

Meanwhile, silver rose 1.9% to $58.19 an ounce, up 3.1% for the week after touching a record $58.98 on Wednesday.

"Silver is on track for a second weekly gain thanks to strong inflows into exchange-traded funds," while rate cut expectations and a softer dollar have also powered the upside, Otunuga added.

Silver has surged about 101% so far this year, driven by a structural supply deficit, liquidity concerns and its addition to the U.S. critical minerals list.

Platinum added 0.7% to $1,657.50 and was set for a small weekly gain, while palladium gained 1.2% to $1,466.54 but was poised to end the week lower.

data.title: Palm rises on Dalian strength, posts second weekly gains
data.description:
Malaysian palm oil futures booked a second consecutive weekly gain as the contract rose after a two-session slide, supported by strength in Dalian and Chicago vegetable oils.

The benchmark palm oil contract for February delivery on the Bursa Malaysia Derivatives Exchange rose 47 ringgit, or 1.14%, to 4,152 ringgit ($1,010.96)a metric ton at closing.

The contract rose 0.92% for the week.

"Bursa Malaysia crude palm oil futures were seen trading higher today on bargain buying following a bullish recovery in Dalian palm olein futures and expectation of a resumption in Indian palm oil buying after washouts of some soyoil shipments," said Anilkumar Bagani, commodity research head at Sunvin Group, a Mumbai-based brokerage.

Dalian's most-active soyoil contract (DBYcv1) gained 0.17%, while its palm oil contract rose 0.94%. Soyoil prices on the Chicago Board of Trade (BOc2) climbed 0.19%.

Palm oil tracks price movements of rival edible oils as it competes for a share of the global vegetable oils market.

Indian refiners have cancelled about 70,000 tons of crude soyoil scheduled for delivery between December and January, as rising global prices and a weaker rupee made local soyoil cheaper than imports, four trade sources told Reuters.

However, the risk of higher Malaysian palm oil stocks by November-end and lower Indonesian palm oil export taxes in December was seen capping the gain in Malaysian palm futures, Bagani added.

Palm oil may retest resistance at 4,192 ringgit per tonne, as it has stabilised around support at 4,093 ringgit, according to Reuters technical analyst Wang Tao.

($1 = 4.1070 ringgit)

data.title: German Factory Orders Rise Further — 2nd Update
data.description:
By Don Nico Forbes

German manufacturing orders rose again in October, driven by an uptick in domestic demand and signaling continued resilience in the factory sector amid trade uncertainty.

Total orders were up 1.5% on month, rising further after a revised 2.0% jump in September, statistics agency Destatis said Friday. A consensus of economists polled by The Wall Street Journal expected a rise of 0.5%.

The second consecutive monthly rise in orders follows four months of declines as manufacturing struggled to adapt to trade uncertainty following the imposition of U.S. tariffs. As a member of the European Union, Germany faces a 15% tariff on most of its goods exports to the U.S.

"Today's figures confirm that the economy in the industrial sector has stabilized," Ralph Solveen at Commerzbank Research said.

Meanwhile in France, industrial production experienced a slowdown in October, rising by 0.2% against a 0.7% gain in the prior month. Electronics manufacturing slipped, while coking and refining surged.

"These figures paint a mixed picture of French industry. After a rebound since June, momentum appears to be fading, and business sentiment does not point to an imminent recovery," said Charlotte de Montpellier, senior economist at ING.

Looking to 2026, France stands to gain from rising global military spending as the European country most exposed to this trend, de Montpellier said. Still, political and fiscal uncertainty remains a significant downside risk, she added.

In Germany, the pickup in manufacturing orders was mostly attributable to a large-scale order in the other transport equipment sector, which includes aircraft, ships, trains and military vehicles, Destatis said. Airbus, which has a factory in Germany and experienced a jump in orders for October, was likely in part behind this figure, according to Pantheon Macroeconomics.

Excluding large-scale orders, total orders rose 0.5%.

A surge in basic metal orders also contributed to the rise, alongside an uptick in orders of capital goods. Meanwhile, electrical equipment orders slipped.

Domestic orders jumped 9.9% in October, amid hopes that an injection of government funds for infrastructure and defense could boost industry and drive domestic demand.

Foreign orders were down 4%.

A spending package for civil and defense projects was agreed to by German lawmakers in March, paving the way for up to 1 trillion euros ($1.165 trillion) in investment. The move reflects a recognition of Germany's weakening export power amid high U.S. tariffs and fierce competition from China, with a focus on higher borrowing and investment, and an emphasis on domestic demand over exports.

"Next year, the expansionary fiscal policy is likely to lead to a slight upturn, without, however, solving the structural problems facing German industry and the German economy as a whole," Solveen said.

Germany's economy, largely stagnant since 2019, is forecast to grow by 1.2% in both 2026 and 2027, according to the European Union.

The eurozone's economic performance for the third quarter was revised up to 0.3% on Friday, a slightly faster pace than the prior quarter and driven by a rebound in investment.

Write to Don Nico Forbes at don.forbes@wsj.com

data.title: Saudi Arabia – PBs for Arana/Hadda STPs
data.description:
By Rod Morrison

Saudi Water Partnership Company has named the Miahona/Marafiq/Buhur team as the preferred bidder on the 250,000m3/day Arana sewage treatment project with a bid of SR1.35/m3 (US$0.36.m3) and the Metito/Skybridge/EtihadWE team as the preferred bidder on the 100,000m3/day Hadda scheme with a bid of SR2.354/m3. The Metito/Skybridge/EtihadWE team is the reserve bidder on the Aranah scheme with a bid of SR1.505m3/day and the Miahona/Marafiq/Buhur team is the reserve bidder on the Hadda scheme with a bid of SR2.599m3/day.

With each project including a complete treated sewage effluent reuse system, this represents 31% of the levelised cost of the preferred bidder for the Arana scheme and 27% of the levelised cost of the preferred bidder for the Hadda scheme.

The Arana scheme could be expanded to 500,000m3/day and the Hadda scheme could be expanded to 250,000m3/day. The schemes are backed by 25-year concessions after construction. Arana is due online in Q3 2028 and Hadda in Q2 2028.

The other bidders were GS Inima, Alfanar/CWS and Hassan Allam/Tawzea. GS Inima bid SR1.588m3/day for Arana and SR2.717m3/day for Hadda. Alfanar/CWS bid SR1.849m3/day for Arana and SR 2.999m3/day for Hadda. Hassan Allam/Tawzea bid SR2.634m3/day for Arana and SR3.749m3/day for Hadda.

data.title: Electrolux Sales Target Points to a Shift in Growth — Market Talk
data.description: Electrolux announced a new 4% organic sales growth target, which is meaningfully ahead of the roughly 2% embedded in consensus through 2030, Citi analysts Martin Wilkie and Avinash Mundhra write. The new target points to a strategic tilt to growth versus productivity, Citi adds. Margin targets were maintained. "Valuation remains depressed versus history, and the successful delivery of targets could drive substantial upside." That said, Citi sees improving consumer confidence and spending as important requirements too. Citi rates Electrolux at neutral with a 70 Swedish kronor target price. Shares fall 1.4% to 61.22 kronor. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)
